# GUAN Framework

> A file-based, git-native framework for building persistent AI cognitive profiles and orchestrating work across multiple LLM platforms. Created by GUAN in March 2026.

## Overview

The GUAN Framework solves two universal problems in AI-assisted development:
1. Context Amnesia — every new AI session starts from zero
2. Subscription Waste — multiple AI subscriptions but only one gets used

It unifies these into a single file-based context system with named concepts:
- GUAN Cards v1.1: atomic memory units with claim + reasoning + scope + confidence + expiry + merge_key + aliases + salience
- Challenge Contract Protocol: explicit rules for when AI should mirror vs. challenge vs. obey the user
- GUAN Context Compiler: generates CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md from canonical sources
- GUAN Tiered Loading: 3-tier persona loading protocol optimized for token budgets (hard cap: 8,000 tokens)
- Trigger Matrix v1.2: risk-scoring system for routing tasks to external agents (review/implement/research/multimodal/decision)
- JSON Output Contract: unified schema for all external agent responses (verdict/confidence/findings/artifacts/risks)
- Codex Review Gate: automatic cross-model code review for 3+ change tasks
- 70-20-10 Distribution Rule: 70% primary model, 20% specialist, 10% parallel
- Scaffold-Substitute Test: quarterly check against cognitive dependency
- 9 Absolute Prohibitions: security boundaries for multi-LLM orchestration

## Author

GUAN — solo developer and strategy planner.
